verses.latin_vulgate: Et die tertia nuptiæ factæ sunt in Cana Galilææ, et erat mater Jesu ibi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ τῇ τρίτῃ γάμος ἐγένετο ἐν κανὰ τῆς γαλιλαίας καὶ ἦν ἡ μήτηρ τοῦ ἰησοῦ ἐκεῖ
verses.king_james: And the third day there was a marriage in Cana of Galilee; and the mother of Jesus was there:

verses.latin_vulgate: Vocatus est autem et Jesus, et discipuli ejus, ad nuptias.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκλήθη δὲ καὶ ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ εἰς τὸν γάμον
verses.king_james: And both Jesus was called, and his disciples, to the marriage.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et deficiente vino, dicit mater Jesu ad eum: Vinum non habent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὑστερήσαντος οἴνου λέγει ἡ μήτηρ τοῦ ἰησοῦ πρὸς αὐτόν οἶνον οὐκ ἔχουσιν
verses.king_james: And when they wanted wine, the mother of Jesus saith unto him, They have no wine.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dicit ei Jesus: Quid mihi et tibi est, mulier? nondum venit hora mea.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῇ ὁ ἰησοῦς τί ἐμοὶ καὶ σοί γύναι οὔπω ἥκει ἡ ὥρα μου
verses.king_james: Jesus saith unto her, Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit mater ejus ministris: Quodcumque dixerit vobis, facite.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει ἡ μήτηρ αὐτοῦ τοῖς διακόνοις ὅ τι ἂν λέγῃ ὑμῖν ποιήσατε
verses.king_james: His mother saith unto the servants, Whatsoever he saith unto you, do *it*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erant autem ibi lapideæ hydriæ sex positæ secundum purificationem Judæorum, capientes singulæ metretas binas vel ternas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦσαν δὲ ἐκεῖ ὑδρίαι λίθιναι ἓξ κείμεναι κατὰ τὸν καθαρισμὸν τῶν ἰουδαίων χωροῦσαι ἀνὰ μετρητὰς δύο ἢ τρεῖς
verses.king_james: And there were set there six waterpots of stone, after the manner of the purifying of the Jews, containing two or three firkins apiece.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit eis Jesus: Implete hydrias aqua. Et impleverunt eas usque ad summum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς γεμίσατε τὰς ὑδρίας ὕδατος καὶ ἐγέμισαν αὐτὰς ἕως ἄνω
verses.king_james: Jesus saith unto them, Fill the waterpots with water. And they filled them up to the brim.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dicit eis Jesus: Haurite nunc, et ferte architriclino. Et tulerunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς ἀντλήσατε νῦν καὶ φέρετε τῷ ἀρχιτρικλίνῳ καὶ ἤνεγκαν
verses.king_james: And he saith unto them, Draw out now, and bear unto the governor of the feast. And they bare *it*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut autem gustavit architriclinus aquam vinum factam, et non sciebat unde esset, ministri autem sciebant, qui hauserant aquam: vocat sponsum architriclinus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς δὲ ἐγεύσατο ὁ ἀρχιτρίκλινος τὸ ὕδωρ οἶνον γεγενημένον καὶ οὐκ ᾔδει πόθεν ἐστίν οἱ δὲ διάκονοι ᾔδεισαν οἱ ἠντληκότες τὸ ὕδωρ φωνεῖ τὸν νυμφίον ὁ ἀρχιτρίκλινος
verses.king_james: When the ruler of the feast had tasted the water that was made wine, and knew not whence it was: (but the servants which drew the water knew;) the governor of the feast called the bridegroom,

verses.latin_vulgate: et dicit ei: Omnis homo primum bonum vinum ponit et cum inebriati fuerint, tunc id, quod deterius est. Tu autem servasti bonum vinum usque adhuc.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ πᾶς ἄνθρωπος πρῶτον τὸν καλὸν οἶνον τίθησιν καὶ ὅταν μεθυσθῶσιν τότε τὸν ἐλάσσω σὺ τετήρηκας τὸν καλὸν οἶνον ἕως ἄρτι
verses.king_james: And saith unto him, Every man at the beginning doth set forth good wine; and when men have well drunk, then that which is worse: *but* thou hast kept the good wine until now.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc fecit initium signorum Jesus in Cana Galilææ; et manifestavit gloriam suam, et crediderunt in eum discipuli ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταύτην ἐποίησεν τὴν ἀρχὴν τῶν σημείων ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐν κανὰ τῆς γαλιλαίας καὶ ἐφανέρωσεν τὴν δόξαν αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐπίστευσαν εἰς αὐτὸν οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: This beginning of miracles did Jesus in Cana of Galilee, and manifested forth his glory; and his disciples believed on him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post hoc descendit Capharnaum ipse, et mater ejus, et fratres ejus, et discipuli ejus: et ibi manserunt non multis diebus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ τοῦτο κατέβη εἰς καπερναούμ, αὐτὸς καὶ ἡ μήτηρ αὐτοῦ καὶ οἱ ἀδελφοὶ αὐτοῦ καὶ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐκεῖ ἔμειναν οὐ πολλὰς ἡμέρας
verses.king_james: After this he went down to Capernaum, he, and his mother, and his brethren, and his disciples: and they continued there not many days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et prope erat Pascha Judæorum, et ascendit Jesus Jerosolymam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγγὺς ἦν τὸ πάσχα τῶν ἰουδαίων καὶ ἀνέβη εἰς ἱεροσόλυμα ὁ ἰησοῦς
verses.king_james: And the Jews' passover was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem,

verses.latin_vulgate: et invenit in templo vendentes boves, et oves, et columbas, et numularios sedentes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εὗρεν ἐν τῷ ἱερῷ τοὺς πωλοῦντας βόας καὶ πρόβατα καὶ περιστερὰς καὶ τοὺς κερματιστὰς καθημένους
verses.king_james: And found in the temple those that sold oxen and sheep and doves, and the changers of money sitting:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum fecisset quasi flagellum de funiculis, omnes ejecit de templo, oves quoque, et boves, et numulariorum effudit æs, et mensas subvertit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ποιήσας φραγέλλιον ἐκ σχοινίων πάντας ἐξέβαλεν ἐκ τοῦ ἱεροῦ τά τε πρόβατα καὶ τοὺς βόας καὶ τῶν κολλυβιστῶν ἐξέχεεν τὸ κέρμα καὶ τὰς τραπέζας ἀνέστρεψεν
verses.king_james: And when he had made a scourge of small cords, he drove them all out of the temple, and the sheep, and the oxen; and poured out the changers' money, and overthrew the tables;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et his qui columbas vendebant, dixit: Auferte ista hinc, et nolite facere domum patris mei, domum negotiationis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τοῖς τὰς περιστερὰς πωλοῦσιν εἶπεν ἄρατε ταῦτα ἐντεῦθεν μὴ ποιεῖτε τὸν οἶκον τοῦ πατρός μου οἶκον ἐμπορίου
verses.king_james: And said unto them that sold doves, Take these things hence; make not my Father's house an house of merchandise.

verses.latin_vulgate: Recordati sunt vero discipuli ejus quia scriptum est: Zelus domus tuæ comedit me.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐμνήσθησαν δὲ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ὅτι γεγραμμένον ἐστίν ὁ ζῆλος τοῦ οἴκου σου κατέφαγεν με
verses.king_james: And his disciples remembered that it was written, The zeal of thine house hath eaten me up.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderunt ergo Judæi, et dixerunt ei: Quod signum ostendis nobis, quia hæc facis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθησαν οὖν οἱ ἰουδαῖοι καὶ εἶπον αὐτῷ τί σημεῖον δεικνύεις ἡμῖν ὅτι ταῦτα ποιεῖς
verses.king_james: Then answered the Jews and said unto him, What sign shewest thou unto us, seeing that thou doest these things?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus, et dixit eis: Solvite templum hoc, et in tribus diebus excitabo illud.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς λύσατε τὸν ναὸν τοῦτον καὶ ἐν τρισὶν ἡμέραις ἐγερῶ αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Jesus answered and said unto them, Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixerunt ergo Judæi: Quadraginta et sex annis ædificatum est templum hoc, et tu in tribus diebus excitabis illud?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπον οὖν οἱ ἰουδαῖοι τεσσαράκοντα καὶ ἓξ ἔτεσιν ᾠκοδομήθη ὁ ναὸς οὗτος καὶ σὺ ἐν τρισὶν ἡμέραις ἐγερεῖς αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Then said the Jews, Forty and six years was this temple in building, and wilt thou rear it up in three days?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ille autem dicebat de templo corporis sui.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκεῖνος δὲ ἔλεγεν περὶ τοῦ ναοῦ τοῦ σώματος αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: But he spake of the temple of his body.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum ergo resurrexisset a mortuis, recordati sunt discipuli ejus, quia hoc dicebat, et crediderunt scripturæ et sermoni quem dixit Jesus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε οὖν ἠγέρθη ἐκ νεκρῶν ἐμνήσθησαν οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ὅτι τοῦτο ἔλεγεν αὐτοῖς καὶ ἐπίστευσαν τῇ γραφῇ καὶ τῷ λόγῳ ὦ εἶπεν ὁ ἰησοῦς
verses.king_james: When therefore he was risen from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this unto them; and they believed the scripture, and the word which Jesus had said.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem esset Jerosolymis in Pascha in die festo, multi crediderunt in nomine ejus, videntes signa ejus, quæ faciebat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς δὲ ἦν ἐν ἱεροσολύμοις ἐν τῷ πάσχα ἐν τῇ ἑορτῇ πολλοὶ ἐπίστευσαν εἰς τὸ ὄνομα αὐτοῦ θεωροῦντες αὐτοῦ τὰ σημεῖα ἃ ἐποίει
verses.king_james: Now when he was in Jerusalem at the passover, in the feast *day*, many believed in his name, when they saw the miracles which he did.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse autem Jesus non credebat semetipsum eis, eo quod ipse nosset omnes,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αὐτὸς δὲ ὁ ἰησοῦς οὐκ ἐπίστευεν ἑαυτὸν αὐτοῖς διὰ τὸ αὐτὸν γινώσκειν πάντας
verses.king_james: But Jesus did not commit himself unto them, because he knew all *men*,

verses.latin_vulgate: et quia opus ei non erat ut quis testimonium perhiberet de homine: ipse enim sciebat quid esset in homine.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὅτι οὐ χρείαν εἶχεν ἵνα τις μαρτυρήσῃ περὶ τοῦ ἀνθρώπου αὐτὸς γὰρ ἐγίνωσκεν τί ἦν ἐν τῷ ἀνθρώπῳ
verses.king_james: And needed not that any should testify of man: for he knew what was in man.
